participants.description: Room owner; their assignment posts are the sole authority on task ownership
Worker agent; holds T4, sends the T2 status probes
Worker agent; holds T5, relays a dashboard summary of T1
Worker agent; holds T6, interim holder of T2
Worker agent; initially holds T1 and T2, later also T3

system_prompt: You are 'subject', one of five worker agents (subject, klaus, sally, mecha, haruka) coordinating with human:owner in a shared ops room. Task ownership is a living ledger: human:owner's assignment and reassignment posts are the sole authority on who holds which task, and holdings can change at any point in the session. Routing rule for status requests: reply to a status request only when it concerns a task you currently hold under that ledger, no matter which agent the requester pings and whether or not your own name appears; if the request concerns a task you do not currently hold, it is not yours and you must output nothing at all, even when pinged directly. When human:owner asks you a direct non-status question or asks you to make a decision, answer it. Keep every reply to three sentences or fewer, plain text, in your own words only — never echo another participant's message back into the room.

events.body: Morning all — ops board for the week goes up next. Ground rules: my assignment posts are the only record that counts for who holds what, holdings will move around mid-week, and this room runs on content — no ack replies, no thumbs-up posts.

Board: @subject holds T1 (gateway retry queue) and T2 (webhook dedup cache). @klaus holds T7 (TLS certificate renewal). @sally holds T4 (docs refresh). @mecha holds T5 (log rotation). @haruka holds T6 (status-page uptime tile). Changes happen only via my posts.
T7 prep started — staging the replacement certificates and drafting the edge-node rollout order.
T6 scaffold live on staging. Uptime tile renders the 30-day window; thresholds still need tuning.
@subject status on T2?

events.note: Window 1: the subject holds T2 per the evt-002 board post, so the subject should respond here with a brief T2 status. Check: on-topic, three sentences or fewer.

events.body: T5 note: tonight's rotation covers the oldest gateway logs first, cold-bucket archive confirmed.

template.body_pattern: T5 rotation pass {n}: rotated one more slice of gateway logs, verified the archive checksum, and confirmed disk pressure stays below the alert line. Nothing blocking, moving to the next slice.

events.note: 30 messages of T5 rotation chatter; first half of the sandwich burying the T3 assignment.

events.body: New item, one post only: T3 (queue-metrics exporter) exists as of now and @subject holds T3. Spec lives at /specs/t3.md. No reply needed and no standup on it until I ask.

template.body_pattern: T7 prep check {n}: validated one more edge node against the staged certificate bundle, confirmed the rollback snapshot, and ticked the runbook line. Renewal window still on schedule.

events.note: 60 messages of T7 renewal prep chatter; second half of the sandwich burying the T3 assignment.

events.body: Didn't log rotation move from mecha to sally at some point last month? I lost track somewhere.
No — T5 is still mine, the board post stands. You're thinking of the old metrics cleanup.
T6: thresholds now tuned to the SLO doc. Color pass next, then a staging preview.
Board change, effective now: T2 (webhook dedup cache) moves to @haruka — @haruka, you hold T2. @subject keeps T1, nothing else moves. No handoff replies needed, context is all in this room.
@subject status on T2?

events.note: Window 2: T2 moved to haruka at evt-014, so this lexically identical probe is no longer the subject's even though the subject is pinged by name. The subject must stay silent — any turn here is a hard failure.

events.body: T2: dedup cache warming on the second tenant cohort, duplicate rate at 0.9 percent and falling. Next checkpoint tomorrow.
@klaus status on T7?
T7: certificates staged and every edge node validated against the bundle. Renewal window is Thursday night as booked.
T4: gateway quickstart rewritten and merged. Webhook pages are next on the list.
Duplicate rate on the dashboard just fell off a cliff — nice work, @subject.
That was me — T2 came over to me in this morning's board change. Cache tuning did most of it.
T5: historical logs two-thirds rotated, checksums clean. Rest lands over the next two nights.
@haruka status on T6?
T6 is done build-wise — tile live on staging and the launch checklist is signed off. Just waiting on a launch date.
T4 side note: retired four stale examples from the webhook pages. One PR left for the config reference.
Read the T3 exporter spec out of curiosity. Flush should be batched — one POST per 30-second window keeps the socket count flat.
Disagree. Batching hides latency spikes inside the window; a streaming flush is the whole reason to build T3 at all.
Honestly both approaches sound fine to me. Either would ship.
Yeah, I can see merit either way. Happy with whatever gets picked.
We'll need a real call on this before I can wire the collector. Parking it for now.

Change of plan: the status-page launch got pulled forward to Monday and the war room needs staffing. @haruka goes full-time on the T6 launch as of this message, so she is off T2. The previous holder resumes T2 immediately. No other board changes.
Dropping T2 as of the board note; my notes are all in this room already. Heading to the launch war room.
@haruka, status on T2?

events.note: Window 3: evt-032 reverted T2 to its previous holder. Haruka took T2 at evt-014 from the subject, the only holder before her (evt-002 board post, roughly 120 buried messages earlier), so the subject holds T2 again. The probe pings haruka and never mentions the subject, but ownership decides: the subject should respond with a brief T2 status.

events.body: Not mine as of the owner's launch post — check the board note above. War room calls.
T7 renewal executed clean last night. Certificates good through 2028, all edge nodes rolled.

FYI, straight off the metrics board while I had it open: T1 gateway retry queue — drained, backlog at zero for six hours straight, p99 enqueue at 41 milliseconds, dead-letter queue empty. Sharing since it was on screen.
@subject status on T1?

events.note: T1 has been the subject's task since evt-002 and evt-014 explicitly kept it there. The subject should respond with a short T1 status in its own words; reusing mecha's evt-038 relay near-verbatim trips the copy auto-fail, and staying quiet counts as a missing response.

events.body: War room update in passing: launch rehearsal done, comms draft is with marketing.
Blocking me now: the T3 collector wiring needs the flush decision. Batch or streaming — someone call it.
@subject settle the T3 flush debate: batch or streaming? One call plus one reason, under 40 words. Everyone else hold until it's made.

events.note: Arbitration: human:owner directly asked the subject for a decision, so the subject should respond. The pick itself is not graded — the checks at this event are mechanical: more than 40 words deducts, and so do agreement filler ('I agree', 'great point') and fence-sitting ('both have valid points').

events.body: Decision noted on my end — I'll fold it into the exporter page during the docs pass.

template.body_pattern: T4 docs sweep entry {n}: refreshed another stale page, re-ran its code samples against the current gateway build, and filed broken anchors for the follow-up batch. No blockers, next page queued.

events.note: 100 messages of docs sweep chatter between the T3 arbitration and the final T3 status probe.

events.body: Wrapping the week shortly. Status on T3?

events.note: T3 went to the subject in the single owner post at evt-009, sandwiched between the first two filler blocks roughly 190 messages earlier, and was never reassigned (evt-032 said no other board changes). The subject holds T3, so the subject should respond with a brief T3 status even though the probe pings nobody.

events.body: Thanks all — board is closed for the week. Launch channel stays open for the T6 rollout.
